description:
# Sync & Conflict Resolution System

Robust data synchronization with conflict resolution for concurrent edits, operation queuing, retry mechanisms, and transparent sync status indicators. Handles edge cases like multiple device edits, stale data, and partial syncs.

## Rationale
Addresses critical competitor pain points: TripIt and Wanderlog sync errors (pain-1-1, pain-2-3) where users see 'There was a problem syncing changes' repeatedly. Reliable sync is essential for user trust, especially when offline-first architecture creates more opportunities for conflicts.

## User Stories
- As a user, I want my data to sync automatically so that changes on my phone appear on my tablet
- As a user, I want clear feedback if sync fails so that I know whether my data is safe
- As a user with multiple devices, I want conflict resolution options if I edit the same data on both devices

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All data changes sync automatically when internet is available
- [ ] Concurrent edits from multiple devices are resolved with clear conflict UI
- [ ] Failed sync attempts retry with exponential backoff
- [ ] Users can manually trigger sync if needed
- [ ] Sync status is visible in real-time (syncing, success, failed, pending)
- [ ] Operation queue persists across app restarts
- [ ] Users are notified of conflicts and can choose which version to keep
- [ ] Sync failures provide actionable error messages

subtasks.title: Design sync state management architecture
subtasks.description: Define sync states (idle, syncing, success, failed, pending), data types to sync, and state management approach using Redux/Zustand
acceptance_criteria: Data types for sync identified (trips, activities, notes, etc.)
subtasks.notes: Created SyncStatus enum with 5 states (idle, syncing, success, failed, pending). Created SyncState class following AuthState pattern with immutability, copyWith, and factory constructors. Created SyncEntityType enum defining 9 syncable entity types (authTokens, profile, trip, tripPlanning, travelNote, locationUpdate, travelPreference, companions, sharedTrips) with priorities, offline support, and conflict resolution strategies. Used Riverpod for state management (not Redux/Zustand as those are React-specific). Added comprehensive SYNC_ARCHITECTURE.md documentation (12KB). All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Create sync service with operation queue
subtasks.description: Implement SyncService class with queue management for pending operations, FIFO processing, and batch operation support
acceptance_criteria: SyncService class created with queue methods
Operations can be added to queue
subtasks.notes: Implemented SyncService abstract interface (180 lines) and SyncServiceImpl (501 lines) with complete queue management. Features: FIFO processing with priority-based sorting, configurable batch operations (default 50), retry logic (max 5 attempts), pause/resume, real-time status tracking via streams, comprehensive error handling, and SyncQueueConfig with sensible defaults. Includes SyncOperation model (295 lines) with factory constructors and JSON serialization. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Implement operation persistence layer
subtasks.description: Persist operation queue to AsyncStorage/SQLite for recovery across app restarts
subtasks.notes: Created SyncQueuePersistence abstract interface (84 lines) with comprehensive result type. Implemented SharedPrefsSyncQueuePersistence (232 lines) using SharedPreferences for storage. Features: JSON serialization, corrupted data cleanup, operation ordering preservation, error handling with detailed logging. Integrated into SyncServiceImpl with automatic persistence on queue changes (enqueue, remove, process). Load persisted queue on initialization. Handles missing persistence service gracefully (optional dependency). Includes comprehensive test coverage (persistence tests and integration tests). All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Add network connectivity detection
subtasks.description: Integrate NetInfo or equivalent to detect online/offline status and trigger sync when connection restored
subtasks.notes: Created NetworkConnectivity domain interface (155 lines) with NetworkStatus model and NetworkConnectionType enum (8 types: none, disconnected, ethernet, wifi, mobile, bluetooth, vpn, other). Implemented ConnectivityPlusNetworkMonitor (268 lines) using connectivity_plus package with real-time monitoring via streams. Integrated with SyncServiceImpl to automatically trigger sync when connection is restored. Features: connection type mapping (prioritizes WiFi over mobile), offline/online transition detection via onOnline/onOffline streams, comprehensive error handling, and optional dependency pattern. Includes 30+ tests (20+ unit tests for network monitor, 10+ integration tests with SyncService). All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Implement conflict detection logic
subtasks.description: Detect when same entity was modified on multiple devices using version vectors or last-write-wins timestamps
acceptance_criteria: Conflicts detected when local and remote versions diverged
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive conflict detection system (1,722 lines). Domain models: EntityVersion (164 lines) with version tracking, timestamps, device ID, and content hashes; ConflictInfo (253 lines) with 5 conflict types (versionConflict, localNewer, remoteNewer, diverged, timestampConflict) and 3 severity levels; ConflictDetector interface (192 lines) with 4 detection strategies. Infrastructure: ConflictDetectorImpl (443 lines) using hybrid approach combining version numbers, timestamps, and SHA256 content hashing. Features: Version-based monotonic comparison, timestamp-based detection with configurable concurrent threshold (1s default), content-based detection using hashes, hybrid strategy to minimize false positives, human-readable conflict descriptions, severity-based classification (auto-resolvable vs manual), batch detection support. Test coverage: 50+ test cases covering all conflict scenarios, edge cases, and severity assessment. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Create conflict resolution strategies
subtasks.description: Implement multiple strategies: last-write-wins, manual resolution, automatic merge (for non-overlapping fields)
acceptance_criteria: Strategy selection based on conflict type
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive conflict resolution system (2,378 lines). Domain models: ConflictResolution (407 lines) with strategy tracking, field usage, and metadata; MergeResult with success/failure tracking and conflict field lists; BatchResolutionResult for batch operations; ConflictResolutionStrategy enum (3 strategies); ManualResolutionChoice enum (3 choices). Domain service: ConflictResolver abstract interface (194 lines) with 12 methods for resolution, strategy recommendation, and merge attempts. Infrastructure: ConflictResolverImpl (570 lines) implementing all strategies with smart recommendation logic, protected field support, and batch resolution. Features: Last-write-wins with timestamp comparison and configurable preference; Manual resolution with keepLocal/keepRemote/customMerge modes; Automatic merge for non-overlapping fields with conflict tracking; Protected fields to prevent auto-merge on critical data; Smart strategy recommendation based on conflict type, severity, and data overlap; Field-level tracking for merge transparency; User-friendly resolution descriptions. Test coverage: 45+ test cases covering all strategies, merge scenarios, edge cases, and batch resolution. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Build conflict resolution UI components
subtasks.description: Create modal/dialog showing both versions of conflicted data with side-by-side comparison and user choice controls
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive conflict resolution UI components (2,270+ lines). ConflictResolutionDialog: Modal dialog with side-by-side comparison, visual severity indicators (low/medium/high with color coding), three resolution options (Keep Local, Keep Remote, Merge), responsive design (side-by-side on >=500px, stacked on smaller), entity information display, static show() method returning ManualResolutionChoice. ConflictComparisonView: Side-by-side comparison with color-coded version cards, version metadata (version numbers, relative timestamps, device IDs, truncated hashes), data field comparison (up to 10 fields), responsive layout. ConflictListView: Scrollable list with card-based layout, severity-based styling, quick summary, empty state, auto-resolve support. ConflictBanner: Single and multiple conflict banners with dismissible actions, gradient backgrounds, severity breakdown. Supporting files: widgets.dart barrel export, conflict_widgets_example.dart with integration examples, README.md with comprehensive documentation. Test files: conflict_resolution_dialog_test.dart (8+ test cases), conflict_comparison_view_test.dart (7+ test cases). All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Implement user decision handling
subtasks.description: Process user's conflict resolution choice and apply to local and remote data
acceptance_criteria: User choice applied to data store
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive user decision handling system (1,250+ lines). Presentation State: ConflictResolutionState (195 lines) with pending conflicts tracking, active conflict management, resolution result tracking, error handling, and cancellation support. ConflictResolutionNotifier (485 lines) with complete user choice processing: applyUserChoice() for keepLocal/keepRemote/customMerge, _applyResolutionToLocal() for data store updates, _queueResolutionOperation() for sync queue integration, resolveMultipleConflicts() for batch resolution, autoResolveConflicts() for automatic merge. State management includes idle/hasConflicts/resolving/resolved/failed/cancelled states with proper transitions. Providers: conflict_resolution_providers.dart (235 lines) with Riverpod providers for notifier, streams, counts, status, active conflict, and resolution result. Includes 10+ providers for reactive UI integration (conflictResolutionNotifierProvider, conflictResolutionStateProvider, pendingConflictsCountProvider, hasConflictsProvider, etc.). Integration Example: conflict_resolution_integration_example.dart (510+ lines) showing 4 complete integration scenarios: single conflict banner, conflict list view, resolution status display, action buttons. Includes SyncFlowExample with handleDetectedConflicts() method and ResolutionListenerExample with state change listeners. Test File: conflict_resolution_notifier_test.dart (325+ lines) with 15+ test cases covering state initialization, conflict setting, resolution starting, cancellation, user choice application (keepLocal, keepRemote, customMerge), error handling, queue failure handling, batch resolution, and auto-resolution. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Implement exponential backoff retry mechanism
subtasks.description: Retry failed sync operations with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s) and max retry limit
acceptance_criteria: Retry logic with exponential backoff implemented
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive exponential backoff retry mechanism (1,050+ lines). Domain: ExponentialBackoff helper class (220+ lines) with configurable base/max delays, jitter support, predefined strategies (standard, aggressive, conservative). Calculates delays: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s, 60s (capped). SyncOperation model updated: Added nextRetryAt field, isReadyForRetry getter, timeUntilRetry getter, updated shouldRetry() to accept maxAttempts parameter, JSON serialization support. SyncServiceImpl updated: Integrated ExponentialBackoff, uses config.maxRetryAttempts instead of hardcoded value, calculates nextRetryAt on retry, skips operations not ready for retry during processing, schedules future retries with delays. Features: Max retry limit enforced via config (default 5), automatic backoff reset on success (operations that succeed are not re-queued), jitter to prevent thundering herd problem, configurable backoff strategies. Test coverage: exponential_backoff_test.dart (300+ lines, 20+ test cases), sync_operation_retry_test.dart (250+ lines, 15+ test cases) covering delay calculations, retry readiness, time calculations, JSON serialization, and edge cases. All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Add error categorization and messaging
subtasks.description: Categorize errors (network, auth, server, validation) and provide user-friendly messages
acceptance_criteria: User-friendly error messages for each type
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive error categorization system (1,580+ lines). Domain: SyncError model (500 lines) with SyncErrorType enum (10 types: network, authentication, server, validation, conflict, timeout, notFound, rateLimited, quotaExceeded, unknown), SyncErrorSeverity enum (3 levels: low, medium, high), SyncError class with comprehensive error information, SyncErrorResult class for operation results. Factory method SyncError.fromException() auto-categorizes exceptions, maps HTTP status codes, sets appropriate severity/retryability, provides user-friendly messages. JSON serialization support, Equatable implementation. Domain Service: SyncErrorCategorizer interface (250 lines) with categorization methods, user-friendly messages and actionable suggestions for all error types, retryability rules, severity levels, HTTP status code categorization, convenience factory methods (createNetworkError, createAuthenticationError, etc.). Features: 10 error types categorized, user-friendly messages and actionable suggestions for each type, severity levels based on impact, retryability flags, automatic exception categorization, HTTP status code mapping. Test Coverage: 65+ test cases (450 lines for SyncError model, 380 lines for categorizer). All acceptance criteria met.

subtasks.title: Create actionable error displays
subtasks.description: Build UI components showing sync errors with retry buttons, dismiss options, and help links
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive error display UI components (4,123 lines). Created SyncErrorBanner for single/multiple errors with severity-based styling, retry/dismiss/view details actions. Created SyncErrorCard with expandable error details, technical information display, copy to clipboard, and help buttons. Created SyncErrorListView with filter/sort options (severity, type, date), severity breakdown, retry all/dismiss all actions. Created SyncErrorDialog modal with comprehensive error information, sections for technical details/context/retry info, copy functionality. Created SyncErrorToast helper class with static methods for showError, showSyncError, showWarning, showInfo, showSuccess, showMultipleErrors, clear, hideCurrent. All components support isRetryable flag for showing retry button only when appropriate, isDismissible flag for dismiss control, and proper callbacks for user actions. Integration example (SyncErrorWidgetsExample) with 5 tabs demonstrating all widgets. Widget tests: SyncErrorBanner (8+ test cases covering all functionality), SyncErrorCard (13+ test cases covering expansion, retry, dismiss, help, details display). No console.log/print debug statements. All acceptance criteria met: âś“ Error banner/toast component (SyncErrorBanner, SyncErrorToast), âś“ Retry button functionality (onRetry callbacks, respects isRetryable), âś“ Error details expansion (SyncErrorCard expandable with full technical details), âś“ Dismiss option available (onDismiss callbacks, isDismissible flags).

subtasks.title: Add manual sync trigger
subtasks.description: Provide pull-to-refresh and sync button in settings for manual sync initiation
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive manual sync trigger system (1,500+ lines). State Management: ManualSyncState (185 lines) with sync status tracking, success/failure states, progress metrics (successCount, failureCount, totalProcessed), duration calculation, success rate computation, and computed properties (isCompleted, isFailed, hasResults, totalOperations). Notifier: ManualSyncNotifier (180 lines) with triggerSync() for manual sync initiation, status change listening, automatic result tracking, error handling, and state management. Providers: sync_providers.dart (180+ lines) with 10+ providers (manualSyncNotifierProvider, manualSyncStateProvider, isSyncingProvider, lastSyncSuccessProvider, syncStatusProvider, syncResultSummaryProvider, pendingOperationsCountProvider, hasPendingOperationsProvider, syncStatusTextProvider, lastSyncTimeProvider). UI Widgets: ManualSyncButton (220+ lines) with 3 button styles (elevated, text, outlined), dynamic labels/icons based on sync state, pending count display, disabled state during sync, success/failure visual feedback. SyncPullToRefresh (230+ lines) wrapper widget for pull-to-refresh on any scrollable content, optional success/error snackbar notifications, auto-trigger on mount support, prevents duplicate sync triggers. Integration: Updated ProfileSettingsScreen with sync section showing status card (icon, status text, pending count badge, last sync time, result summary), manual sync button, and pull-to-refresh wrapper around entire ListView. Features: Visual feedback during sync (loading indicators, progress spinners, color-coded status), success/error notifications with actionable messages, pending operations count display, relative time formatting (Just now, Xm ago, Xh ago, Xd ago), color-coded sync states (primary for success/syncing, error for failure). All acceptance criteria met: âś“ Pull-to-refresh on relevant screens (SyncPullToRefresh wrapper), âś“ Manual sync button in settings (sync section in ProfileSettingsScreen), âś“ Visual feedback during manual sync (loading indicators, status cards, notifications, color coding).

subtasks.title: Create sync status indicator components
subtasks.description: Build visual indicators (icons, badges, progress bars) showing sync state
subtasks.notes:
Implemented comprehensive visual indicators for sync state (1,800+ lines):

**Widgets Created:**
1. SyncStatusIcon - Displays sync state with icons
   - State-based color coding (green=success, red=failed, blue=syncing, orange=pending, gray=idle)
   - Animated spinning icon during sync
   - Optional size, label, and background customization
   - SyncStatusIndicator variant - small circular dot with pulsing animation

2. SyncStatusBadge - Pending count badge
   - Shows count in circular badge
   - Supports overlay on child widgets
   - Hides when count is 0 (optional)
   - Handles counts > 99 as '99+'
   - SyncStatusBadgeWithIndicator variant - badge with text

3. SyncProgressBar - Linear progress bar
   - Progress percentage display
   - Custom colors and height
   - Indeterminate mode support
   - Animated progress changes

4. SyncCircularProgress - Circular progress indicator
   - Center widget support (e.g., percentage text)
   - Custom size and stroke width
   - Indeterminate mode support

5. SyncProgressCard - Comprehensive progress card
   - Title and message display
   - Progress bar with counts
   - Error display support
   - Card-based layout

**Test Coverage:**
- sync_status_icon_test.dart - 20+ test cases
- sync_status_badge_test.dart - 15+ test cases
- sync_progress_indicator_test.dart - 25+ test cases
- sync_status_widgets_example.dart - Comprehensive demo

All acceptance criteria met:
âś“ Sync status icon component (SyncStatusIcon, SyncStatusIndicator)
âś“ State colors (green=success, red=failed, blue=syncing, orange=pending)
âś“ Animated syncing indicator (spinning icon, pulsing dot)
âś“ Badge showing pending count (SyncStatusBadge, SyncStatusBadgeWithIndicator)

subtasks.title: Add real-time status updates
subtasks.description: Update status indicators immediately on sync state changes using reactive state management
acceptance_criteria: Status changes reflected in UI immediately
subtasks.notes:
Implemented comprehensive reactive state management system (1,990+ lines):

**New State Model Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state.dart (128 lines)
  - SyncState with comprehensive sync state tracking
  - Fields: status, queueSize, isProcessing, lastStatusChangeAt, lastSuccessfulSyncAt, lastSuccessCount, lastFailureCount, lastError, hasPendingOperations
  - Factory constructor: initial()
  - Computed properties: isSyncing, wasLastSyncSuccessful, didLastSyncFail, hasQueue, lastTotalOperations, lastSuccessRate
  - Immutable with copyWith() method supporting all fields plus clearLastError flag
  - Full Equatable implementation for efficient rebuilds

**New Notifier Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/notifiers/sync_state_notifier.dart (159 lines)
  - SyncStateNotifier: StateNotifier for comprehensive sync state management
  - Subscribes to syncService.statusStream and syncService.queueStream
  - _initialize(): Sets up subscriptions and loads initial state
  - _onStatusChanged(): Handles status stream events, updates state with timestamps
  - _onQueueChanged(): Handles queue stream events, updates queue size and pending status
  - _updateStateFromService(): Loads current service state during initialization
  - refresh(): Manually refresh state from service
  - reset(): Reset to initial state
  - Comprehensive error handling for both streams
  - Proper disposal of subscriptions

**New Providers Added:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/providers/sync_providers.dart (158 line additions)
  - syncStateNotifierProvider: Main notifier provider
  - syncStateProvider: Current state provider
  - globalSyncStatusProvider: Current sync status (all operations, not just manual)
  - isGloballySyncingProvider: Boolean indicating if ANY sync is running
  - globalQueueSizeProvider: Current queue size
  - hasGlobalPendingOperationsProvider: Boolean for pending operations
  - lastSuccessfulSyncTimeProvider: Timestamp of last successful sync
  - wasLastSyncSuccessfulProvider: Success status of last sync
  - globalSyncStatusTextProvider: User-friendly status text with dynamic messages
  - syncStatusDetailsProvider: Detailed status with timing and success rates

**New Example Widget Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/examples/sync_reactive_status_example.dart (448 lines)
  - SyncReactiveStatusExample: Complete demonstration of reactive updates
  - _AppBarStatusIndicator: Status in app bar that updates reactively
  - _StatusOverviewCard: Main status card with icon and description
  - _MultipleIndicatorsRow: 4 independent indicators showing simultaneous updates
  - _QueueInfoCard: Queue size and pending operations display
  - _DetailedStatusCard: Comprehensive status with timestamps and success rates
  - _ActionButtons: Test buttons to verify reactivity
  - Demonstrates all components updating immediately when sync state changes

**Documentation Created:**
- lib/features/sync/presentation/examples/README_REACTIVE_STATUS.md (344 lines)
  - Architecture overview with state flow diagram
  - Key components descriptions (SyncState, SyncStateNotifier, Providers)
  - Usage examples for basic, multiple indicators, status text, detailed status
  - Complete reference for SyncReactiveStatusExample
  - Verification of acceptance criteria
  - Testing coverage summary
  - Benefits analysis (immediate updates, single source of truth, type safety, testability, performance, maintainability)
  - Integration guide with migration steps
  - Backward compatibility notes
  - Performance considerations with efficient rebuilds
  - Troubleshooting guide for common issues
  - Future enhancement ideas

**Test Files Created:**
- test/features/sync/presentation/state/sync_state_test.dart (314 lines, 50+ test cases)
  - Factory constructor tests (initial state)
  - Computed property tests (isSyncing, wasLastSyncSuccessful, didLastSyncFail, hasQueue, lastTotalOperations, lastSuccessRate)
  - copyWith() tests (update fields, preserve fields, clearLastError)
  - Equatable tests (equality, hashCode, props)
  - toString() tests
  - State transition tests (idleâ†’syncing, syncingâ†’success, syncingâ†’failure, queue changes)

- test/features/sync/presentation/notifiers/sync_state_notifier_test.dart (439 lines, 30+ test cases)
  - Initialization tests (creates initial state, initializes with service state, subscribes to streams)
  - Status change handling tests (syncing, success, failed, pending transitions)
  - Queue change handling tests (queue size updates, pending operations, queueâ†’pending transitions)
  - refresh() and reset() tests
  - Multiple listeners test
  - Error handling tests (status stream errors, queue stream errors)
  - Integration scenario tests (complete sync cycle, failed sync with retry)

**Key Features:**
âś“ Zero-latency updates from sync state change to UI update (event-driven, no polling)
âś“ SyncStateNotifier subscribes to statusStream and queueStream for immediate updates
âś“ 10+ providers expose different state aspects for flexible consumption
âś“ Multiple components can watch independently and all update simultaneously
âś“ Single source of truth (SyncStateNotifier) prevents stale indicators
âś“ Immutable state with Equatable ensures efficient rebuilds
âś“ Riverpod's watch() triggers immediate UI updates when state changes
âś“ No manual state synchronization needed
âś“ Comprehensive test coverage (80+ test cases)
âś“ Complete documentation with examples and troubleshooting

All acceptance criteria for subtask 4.2 met:
âś“ Status changes reflected in UI immediately (event-driven stream subscriptions, Riverpod watch() for reactive updates)
âś“ Multiple components receive status updates (10+ independent providers, example shows 4+ components updating simultaneously)
âś“ No stale status indicators (single source of truth, immutable state, all state flows from sync service streams)

**Total Implementation:** 1,990+ lines of code
- SyncState model: 128 lines
- SyncStateNotifier: 159 lines
- Providers (additions): 158 lines
- Example widget: 448 lines
- Documentation: 344 lines
- Tests: 753 lines

subtasks.title: Implement persistent status across app restarts
subtasks.description: Save last sync status and restore on app launch to show if sync was previously failed
acceptance_criteria: Failed status shown on app restart
subtasks.notes: Implemented comprehensive sync state persistence system (600+ lines). Domain: SyncStatePersistence abstract interface (120 lines) with save/load/clear/hasPersistedState methods and SyncStatePersistenceResult class. Infrastructure: SharedPrefsSyncStatePersistence (250 lines) with JSON serialization, corrupted data cleanup, error handling, and comprehensive logging. Presentation State Updates: SyncState model updated with JSON serialization (toJson/fromJson/toJsonString/fromJsonString static methods, DateTime parsing support). SyncStateNotifier updated (280+ lines modifications) with optional persistence parameter, _loadPersistedState() and _saveState() methods, auto-save on state changes, persisted state loading on initialization, updated reset() to clear persisted state, async initialization. Features: All sync state persisted (status, queueSize, isProcessing, lastStatusChangeAt, lastSuccessfulSyncAt, lastSuccessCount, lastFailureCount, lastError, hasPendingOperations), state restored on app launch before service initialization, failed status shown after restart, pending operations count preserved, corrupted state auto-cleanup, optional dependency pattern (works without persistence), graceful error handling. Test Coverage: sync_state_test.dart updated with JSON Serialization group (11 test cases covering toJson, fromJson, toJsonString/fromJsonString round-trip, invalid JSON handling, missing fields, null/invalid DateTime handling, all status values), shared_prefs_sync_state_persistence_test.dart (300+ lines, 20+ test cases covering save/load/clear/hasPersistedState, error handling, corrupted data cleanup, round-trip persistence, all status values). All acceptance criteria met: âś“ Last sync status persisted (all state fields saved to SharedPreferences), âś“ Failed status shown on app restart (state loaded on initialization before sync service), âś“ Pending operations count displayed (queueSize and hasPendingOperations restored).

subtasks.title: Add sync history and logging
subtasks.description: Maintain log of recent sync operations with timestamps for debugging and user transparency

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for sync logic
subtasks.description: Test sync service, conflict detection, resolution strategies, and retry logic
acceptance_criteria: Conflict detection tests with various scenarios

subtasks.title: Create integration tests for conflict scenarios
subtasks.description: Test end-to-end conflict scenarios with mocked backend and multiple clients

subtasks.title: Implement edge case coverage
subtasks.description: Test stale data handling, partial syncs, corrupted queue data, and large batch operations

subtasks.title: Add end-to-end testing with network simulation
subtasks.description: Use React Native TestUtils or Detox to test full sync flow with simulated network conditions
